title: Category：AI PM Identity / Workflow Thinking
summary:
Category：AI PM Identity / Workflow Thinking
Created：2025-12-01
Linked：RTI-1.4（AI-Native PM）、RTI-3.1（Workflow Architect）
Summary
你在這篇文章中闡述 AI PM 真正的能力不是 Prompting，而是設計一個可被 LLM 穩定執行的「系統」。MentorFlow v0.8 展示了你從“模型回答”到“模型在 workflow 運行”的思維躍遷。
Key Points
AI PM 的核心不是 prompt，而是 execution system 的設計。
你透過 MentorFlow 重新定義 Lesson Flow：Goal → Concept → Question → Feedback → Reinforcement → Next Step。
AI-native UX（streaming, auto-read, multi-mode）是穩定體驗的關鍵。
你採用 plug-in architecture，使 MentorFlow 能跨版本擴展，而非一次性 dem

title: Category：AI PM Growth Framework
summary:
Category：AI PM Growth Framework
Created：2025-11-27
Linked：RTI-1.4（AI-Native PM Growth）、RTI-1.6（10 Abilities）
Summary
你提出的「三線式成長系統」是 AI PM 的有效學習模型：Build（實作）、Think & Write（反思）、Learn Theory（概念）。此模式建立一條能不斷迭代與深化的能力飛輪。
Key Points
Line 1：Build → MentorFlow 成為你的 AI 系統實驗室。
Line 2：Think & Write → 把實驗轉成 PM 思維。
Line 3：Learn Theory → 只學 PM 用得到的 AI 概念。
三線形成循環：做 → 想 → 學 → 再做。
Source Citation
The “3 Lines Learning System” I’m using to grow into an AI PM
Trying to go deeper into AI as a PM has been overwhelming.

title: **How MentorFlow took its first step from 2D → 3D.**
summary:
**How MentorFlow took its first step from 2D → 3D.**
今天，我做了一件有點不尋常的事。
在進入 MentorFlow v0.9（Evaluation Engine）之前，
我先完成了一個支線任務：
👉 **讓講課的 AI，不再只停留在 2D。**
👉 **而是站進一個 3D 世界裡，用三種語言教課。**
這是 **MentorFlow 的第一個 3D Interface Layer**。
版本名稱：**MVP 0.1**（獨立於主版本線）
---
# **1️⃣ Why I Built This (Before v0.9)**
MentorFlow 的 Lecture Engine（語音版教學引擎）越做越成熟。
但我一直有個問題：
> 「如果 AI 是未來的企業導師，它真的需要永遠停留在平面嗎？」
企業訓練不會永遠是文件、影片、測驗。
AI-native training 一定會走向 **沉浸式、多語言、互動式**。
所以我想驗證一件事：
📌 **Lecture Engine 能不能在不改架構的情況下直接輸出到 3D？**
📌 **跨

title: Category：AI PM System Reasoning / Agent Workflows
summary:
Category：AI PM System Reasoning / Agent Workflows
Created：2025-12-04
Linked：RTI-1.5（Course Gap）、RTI-1.6（10 Abilities）、RTI-3.1（Workflow Architect）
Summary
你指出 AI PM 正從 Prompts → Agents → Systems 的方向演化。AWS re:Invent 只是揭露了業界趨勢：AI PM 必須設計“可執行任務的系統”，而不只是調整 prompt。
Key Points
LLM 1.0 → 回答；LLM 2.0 → 執行任務。
MentorFlow 在本質上已經是 agent orchestration（Teach → Correct → Ask → Guide）。
Future AI = multi-agent systems + workflow design。
你已從“prompt PM”進化為“system PM”。
Source Citation
The Future of AI PM Is Clearer A

title: From Game Prototype to Real Business MVP — Built in Just One Week with AI In the past, building an MVP as a Product Mana
summary: From Game Prototype to Real Business MVP — Built in Just One Week with AI In the past, building an MVP as a Product Manager required coordinating at least one designer and one engineer, and typically took one to two weeks. Any requirement change meant additional rework and slower iteration.

title: MentorFlow v0.6 is live 🚀 and this is the first version that starts to feel like a real product.
summary: MentorFlow v0.6 is live 🚀 and this is the first version that starts to feel like a real product. For the past weekend, I’ve been rebuilding the entire interface, moving from a simple HTML demo to a real product structure using React + Tailwind + shadcn/ui.

summary: MentorFlow v0.7 is live — and this version adds something special: your own knowledge. (Try it : https://lnkd.in/eQe_DJ2K) For the first time, MentorFlow can learn from the user.

title: AI PM as a systems designer — the real skill isn’t “prompting,” but turning an idea into a working AI system.
summary: AI PM as a systems designer — the real skill isn’t “prompting,” but turning an idea into a working AI system. MentorFlow v0.8 is my latest proof.

title: LTI-4.1 — My Favourite AI Books (and Why They Matter to PMs)
summary:
LTI-4.1 — My Favourite AI Books (and Why They Matter to PMs)
Category：AI PM Learning / Strategy
Created：2025-12-02
Linked：RTI-1.6（10 Abilities）、RTI-2.1（Tacit Knowledge）
Summary
你介紹了三本深化 AI PM 思維的書，涵蓋 RAG、evaluation、AI decision frameworks 與 workflow 理論，強調“為什麼 AI 有效運作”比“怎麼用模型”更重要。
Key Points
《Hands-On LLMs》強化 RAG 與 evaluation 的實務理解。
《AI Product Manager’s Handbook》專注 problem framing 與錯誤成本。
《Power and Prediction》指出 AI 本質是 workflow 轉換，而非工具升級。
Source Citation
My favourite AI books: hashtag#LinkedInNew

title: **Most AI products don’t fail because the model is weak.**
summary:
**Most AI products don’t fail because the model is weak.**
**They fail because we can’t explain quality regressions.**
Over the past few days, I finished v0.90 to v0.91 of the evaluation system for MentorFlow, an AI teaching product I’m building. This work wasn’t about improving demo quality.

MentorFlow v0.94.1: What It Takes to Move From NO-GO to GO
In v0.94, we blocked a release with a 92% average score. That decision raised a fair question:
Did we just describe the problem
or did we actually fix it?

title: When AI Knowledge Changes Behaviour, Governance Becomes a PM Problem
summary: Explores how AI systems that change user behaviour inherently shift governance responsibility to product management, making judgement a product concern rather than a policy afterthought.

title: 2026: AI Will Stop Being Impressive, and Start Being Accountable
summary: Argues that the next phase of AI evolution is defined not by performance or demos, but by accountability, traceability, and governance of AI-driven decisions.

title: Beyond Pass/Fail: Lessons from Risk-Tiered AI Evaluation
summary: Shows why binary pass/fail evaluation is insufficient for AI systems and introduces risk-tiered evaluation as a foundation for governance and judgement.

title: Accountability Is a Design Choice, Not a Role
summary: Establishes accountability as an explicit design decision embedded in system workflows, rather than a responsibility assigned to a person or role.

title: “It Works” Isn’t Enough: How PMs Decide When to Say NO
summary: Examines how product managers make NO-GO decisions in AI systems, emphasizing judgement, risk ownership, and governance beyond technical correctness.
